title: The Fadiman Protocol Explained: Every Third Day Microdosing Schedule
body:
The Fadiman Protocol Explained: Every Third Day Microdosing Schedule

So, you’ve heard about microdosing psychedelics and you’re wondering what the fuss is about. Maybe you’ve tried a few different schedules and felt a little lost, or you want to start with something that’s been tested and talked about a lot. Enter the Fadiman Protocol - a simple, easy-to-follow microdosing schedule that’s gained popularity because it works for many people. But what exactly is it? How does it work? And does it really make a difference? Let’s unpack all that, step by step.

Who is James Fadiman, Anyway?

James Fadiman is a researcher and writer who’s been studying psychedelics since the 1960s. He’s one of the first people to document what microdosing looks like in practice, especially after psychedelics made a comeback in recent years. Fadiman’s work isn’t about tripping hard or seeking intense spiritual experiences. Instead, he focused on tiny doses - usually about one-tenth to one-twentieth of a standard dose - taken regularly to gently boost mood, creativity, focus, and well-being without overwhelming your day.

The Fadiman Protocol is his signature microdosing schedule. If you’ve ever heard the phrase “microdose every third day,” that’s his method.

What Does “Every Third Day” Mean?

The schedule is pretty straightforward. You take a microdose on Day 1, skip Day 2, skip Day 3, then microdose again on Day 4. Right?! It sounds simple, but there’s a method to the madness.

  • Day 1: Take your microdose in the morning.
  • Day 2: No dose. Pay attention to how you feel.
  • Day 3: No dose. Continue observing any changes in mood, creativity, or energy.
  • Day 4: Microdose again.

And you keep repeating that cycle.

Why skip two days? Here’s the thing: Fadiman found that taking a microdose every day can lead to tolerance, where your body and brain get used to the substance and the effects diminish. By spacing doses out, you give your system time to reset. This way, the microdose stays effective and you avoid building up a tolerance.

How Much Should You Take?

Microdoses are typically around 1/10th to 1/20th of a standard psychedelic dose. For example, if a full dose of psilocybin mushrooms is around 2 grams of dried mushrooms, your microdose might be 0.1 to 0.2 grams. For LSD, a full dose is often around 100 micrograms, so a microdose would be 10 to 20 micrograms.

Everyone’s sensitivity varies. What’s tiny for one person might be noticeable for another. Start low and slow. Keep a journal - honestly, it helps more than you think. Logging your dose, mood, energy, and focus can reveal patterns you’d miss otherwise. Speaking of journals, if you want to get serious about tracking your experience, consider a therapy journal with guided prompts (paid link). It’s designed to help you reflect deeply and consistently.

What Happens on the Dosing Day?

On the day you microdose, you’ll likely notice subtle shifts rather than dramatic changes. Some people feel a gentle boost in energy, a clearer mind, or a slight uplift in mood. Creativity might flow easier. Social interactions could feel more natural. But it’s not a rollercoaster. It’s more like a soft push forward, not a shove.

Does that land? If you’re expecting to see the world in neon colors or go on a wild trip, you’ll be disappointed. The Fadiman Protocol is designed for gentle, steady progress. That’s also why it’s critical to dose in the morning. You want the effects during your waking hours; it’s about integrating this new way of thinking and feeling into your daily life, not escaping it.

What About the Off Days?

Days two and three - no dosing, but plenty of paying attention. This is when you notice how the microdose lingers. Some people say they feel the afterglow, like a softening of anxiety or an easier flow in problem-solving. Others notice subtle shifts in sleep or emotional regulation. The off days help you track these changes and prevent tolerance.

For example, you might find that your mind stays sharper or your mood stays steadier even when you’re not actively microdosing. That’s part of the protocol’s appeal: it’s not about chasing a high every day but about improving baseline functioning over time.

Who Should Try the Fadiman Protocol?

Look, microdosing isn’t for everyone. If you’re prone to anxiety, have a history of psychosis, or take medications that interact badly with psychedelics (like certain antidepressants), tread carefully and always consult a healthcare provider. But if you’re curious about gentle ways to improve creativity, focus, or mood, the Fadiman Protocol offers a straightforward starting place.

It’s also great for people who want to avoid burnout. Continuous dosing can be intense, but a spaced-out schedule feels manageable and sustainable. Some people find they can keep this up for months without feeling drained or overwhelmed.

Some Real-Life Tips for Following the Fadiman Protocol

  • Set reminders. It’s easy to forget which day you’re on. Use your phone calendar or a habit tracker.
  • Start with a small dose. You can always increase slightly but can’t take less once it’s in your system.
  • Journal. Write down what you notice each day. Even small shifts matter.
  • Stay hydrated and well-rested. Psychedelics can amplify your baseline state, so keep your body cared for. A good night’s sleep matters - maybe even invest in a silk sleep eye mask (paid link) for better rest.
  • Be patient. Effects can be subtle and take a few cycles to feel solid improvements.

What Does the Research Say?

Truth is, scientific research on microdosing is still emerging, but early findings are intriguing. A study published in Nature found that microdosing psychedelics was associated with small but meaningful improvements in mood and cognitive function, although placebo effects can’t be ruled out completely. Meanwhile, Johns Hopkins Medicine highlights that more controlled trials are needed to understand the benefits and risks fully.

What’s fascinating is that anecdotal reports, including Fadiman’s extensive surveys, show many people experiencing enhanced creativity, emotional balance, and focus. The Fadiman Protocol’s spaced schedule likely helps avoid tolerance and negative side effects, making it a practical choice for many.

How to Know if the Fadiman Protocol is Working for You

Keep an eye on your baseline mood, productivity, and creativity over time. Are you more engaged at work? Do you solve problems more easily? Are your anxiety and stress levels dropping? Notice these things without forcing an answer. The results are usually slow and steady, not sudden breakthroughs.

It helps to have some objective measures, too. That’s where tools like a WHOOP HRV monitor (paid link) can be useful. HRV (heart rate variability) tracks stress and recovery, giving you physical data on how your body is responding to your microdosing practice.

Potential Downsides and What to Watch For

Microdosing isn’t risk-free. Some people report jitteriness, anxiety, or difficulty sleeping. Remember, you’re putting a psychoactive substance into your body. If you notice any negative side effects, stop dosing and talk to a healthcare professional.

Also, the quality and consistency of your psychedelic source matter a lot. Dosage can vary wildly depending on growing conditions, batch, or chemical purity. If you’re using psilocybin mushrooms, for example, make sure you’re weighing doses precisely with a scale sensitive to 0.01 grams. Consistency is key to following the Fadiman Protocol accurately.

Integrating the Fadiman Protocol into Your Life

Microdosing with the Fadiman Protocol isn’t just about the dose. It’s about how you live your life while microdosing. Many find that pairing it with mindful habits like journaling, meditation, or regular exercise deepens the benefits. You don’t have to overhaul your life overnight, but small changes can add up.

If you want some ideas to support your mental health journey alongside microdosing, check out Mindful Mornings and Microdosing. It’s a practical guide to starting your day with intention, which can amplify the effects of your microdose.

And if you want to learn more about me and my work, head over to kalesh.love. I write and teach about conscious healing and psychedelic wellness, always aiming for clarity and grounded advice.

Final Thoughts

The Fadiman Protocol is straightforward, accessible, and supported by decades of anecdotal evidence. It’s a gentle way to integrate psychedelics into your routine without overwhelming your system. The every-third-day schedule gives your brain a chance to reset and keeps your experience fresh.

Look, if you’re curious about microdosing, give this protocol a try with patience and care. Track your experience, stay mindful, and listen closely to your body and mind. And don’t forget the practical side: good sleep, hydration, and maybe a little tech assistance like the tools mentioned above.

Does that sound doable? I hope so. Remember, microdosing isn’t magic, but it can be a helpful tool in your wellness kit when approached with respect and attention.

Disclaimer: This content is for educational purposes only and does not constitute medical advice. Always consult a qualified healthcare provider before making changes to your health regimen.
